_note: Audit record of all routing attempts during Set 016 Session 1. The original _route_consultation.py crashed on a Windows cp1252 console encoding error after Gemini's call succeeded but before this JSON dump was written; this file is reconstructed from the metrics record and provider-response files on disk.

gemini-2.5-pro.notes: Clean completion via raw API. Substantial 21KB response covering all five questions plus caveats. See router-metrics.jsonl for the canonical metrics row.

gpt-5-4-via-codex.notes: Run manually by operator via Codex after raw-API attempts timed out. Token/cost data not captured because the call did not pass through ai_router.providers.call_model. The IDE-integrated routing turned out to be load-bearing: Codex inspected the actual harvester repo state, which is what produced GPT's empirical advantages over Gemini-via-API (noticed dirty main + 36-commit gap, empirically tested git clean -fdx -n on Git 2.51). See docs/case-studies/cross-provider-collaboration-spike-016.md for the full discussion.

gpt-5-4-via-api-attempts.total_elapsed_seconds_approx: unknown (script error before timing was captured)
gpt-5-4-via-api-attempts.failure: RuntimeError: API call failed after 3 attempts: The read operation timed out
gpt-5-4-via-api-attempts.notes: First attempt with default 300s timeout. All three retries (per provider config) hit the read timeout.

gpt-5-4-via-api-attempts.failure: RuntimeError: API call failed after 2 attempts: The read operation timed out
gpt-5-4-via-api-attempts.notes: Second attempt with bumped 1200s (20-min) timeout per attempt. Both attempts still hit the read timeout. This is anomalous — past metrics show GPT-5.4 with reasoning_effort:high typically completes similar prompts in 40-60 seconds. Cause is unclear: candidates include OpenAI API instability on the day, pathological reasoning loops on the specific prompt shape, or network buffering causing a streaming-style response to look like a hung request to a synchronous httpx client. The eventual successful path (GPT-5.4 medium via Codex) avoided the issue entirely.

_open_issues_for_followup: Windows cp1252 console encoding bug in _route_consultation.py (now deleted) — any future routing helper script must replace U+2192 (→) and other non-cp1252 characters in print statements, or set PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8.
GPT-5.4 reasoning_effort:high timeout on long structured prompts — needs further investigation if reproduced; default to reasoning_effort:medium for advisory/structured tasks until understood.
